claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex, he's 13 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex? That is, do you live with him and are you responsible for paying for things like his food, clothes, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for him - I take care of his food, clothes, and all his everyday needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training (for example, at school), or does this not apply because he's only 13?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's just 13, so he's still at school doing his regular classes - nothing like college or advanced courses yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions:

**Do you (or your partner if you have one) currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. That's an important detail. Let me ask:

**Are you or your partner covered by UK National Insurance contributions (for example, because you work for a UK employer or the Crown), or are you a Crown servant or member of the armed forces posted overseas?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not working for a UK employer or the Crown, and I'm not in the armed forces either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me gather just a bit more detail to complete the assessment.

**Are you or your partner receiving certain benefits or pensions from the UK (such as State Pension, contribution-based benefits, or industrial injuries benefits), or are you working for an international organization or the EU?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any UK benefits or pensions, and I'm not working for an international organization or the EU.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules before giving you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the official guidance I've reviewed, I can now provide you with an assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

Here's why: While you are responsible for Alex, he lives with you, and you meet the care requirements, the key issue is that **you do not currently live in the UK**. The eligibility rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**.

There are some exceptions for people living abroad, such as:
- Crown servants or members of the armed forces posted overseas
- People working for UK employers abroad and covered by UK National Insurance
- People receiving certain UK benefits or pensions

However, you've confirmed that none of these exceptions apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and lives with the claimant who is responsible for his care, food, clothes, and everyday needs. He is in regular school education. However, the claimant does not currently live in the UK. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The claimant may be able to get Child Benefit if they go to live in certain countries or if they're a Crown servant, but the claimant confirmed they are not working for a UK employer or the Crown, not in the armed forces, not receiving UK benefits or pensions, and not working for an international organization or the EU. Without living in the UK or meeting one of the specific exceptions for living abroad (Crown servant, armed forces, or certain other qualifying conditions), the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for their 13-year-old son Alex who lives with them and is in regular school education. However, the claimant does not currently live in the UK and does not meet any of the exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit (such as being a Crown servant, member of the armed forces, or receiving certain UK benefits). The UK residency requirement is a fundamental eligibility criterion that is not met in this case.

The claimant may be able to get Child Benefit if they go to live in certain countries or if they're a Crown servant, but the claimant confirmed they are not working for a UK employer or the Crown, not in the armed forces, not receiving UK benefits or pensions, and not working for an international organization or the EU. Without living in the UK or meeting one of the specific exceptions for living abroad (Crown servant, armed forces, or certain other qualifying conditions), the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for their 13-year-old son Alex who lives with them and is in regular school education. However, the claimant does not currently live in the UK and does not meet any of the exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit (such as being a Crown servant, member of the armed forces, or receiving certain UK benefits). The UK residency requirement is a fundamental eligibility criterion that is not met in this case.
